vulnerability.title: Disable Host-Based Authentication
vulnerability.description: SSH's cryptographic host-based authentication is more secure than .rhosts authentication. However, it is not recommended that hosts unilaterally trust one another, even within an organization. The default SSH configuration disables host-based authentication. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for HostbasedAuthentication. To explicitly disable host-based authentication, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: HostbasedAuthentication no
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*HostbasedAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*HostbasedAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*HostbasedAuthentication\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "

triage_decision.reason: Disabling host-based authentication is a standard SSH hardening measure that aligns with STIG recommendations. It does not affect primary SSH access methods (e.g., password or key-based) and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage, as the default configuration already disables this feature.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential misconfiguration if remediation script is applied incorrectly; Could affect legacy applications relying on host-based authentication

commands_executed: grep -i "HostbasedAuthentication" /etc/ssh/sshd_config || echo "Not found in main config"
if [ -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf ]; then cat /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf; else echo "FILE_NOT_FOUND"; fi

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about disabling host-based authentication in SSH. According to the context, I need to:

1. Check if there's already a configuration for HostbasedAuthentication in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf
2. If it exists, ensure it's set to "no"
3. If it doesn't exist, add it

The recommendation suggests creating a file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf if needed, but the description says to add or correct the line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf.

Let me first read the file to see the current state.
